title: Kids Art Center Stools
summary: A small splay-legged plywood stool with a seat back, sized to pair with a kids' art center or table.
description:
Every leg on this stool carries a compound 5° bevel and miter, which is what gives it a confident outward splay instead of standing straight up and down — small, but it's the detail that makes this read as a real piece of furniture rather than a plain box on legs. Mark the inside corner at the top of each leg and the outside corner at the bottom before you start; a 5° angle is genuinely hard to judge once you're mid-assembly, and that mark is what keeps every leg oriented the same way.

How you attach the seat back depends on your finish: pocket holes are quick and invisible under paint, but for a stained finish, driving carefully placed screws up from underneath keeps the show faces completely clean.

tools.note: Needs to hold a compound bevel and miter for the legs.

cutList.note: 5° bevel and miter on both ends, parallel; measured short point to long point.
5° miter on both ends, NOT parallel; measured short point to short point.

steps.title: Mark and build the leg sets
steps.body:
Mark the inside corner at the top and the outside corner at the bottom of every leg — a 5° bevel is hard to judge once you're mid-build, and this mark keeps every leg oriented correctly.

With the inside corners facing in, attach an apron to two legs; repeat to build a second leg set. Use the 3/4" pocket-hole setting and 1-1/4" pocket-hole screws, with the holes drilled in the aprons.

Also drill a set of 3/4" pocket holes facing upward in these aprons now, for attaching the seat in a later step.

steps.title: Join the leg sets
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes on either end of the remaining apron pieces, also facing upward for attaching the seat later. Attach these to the two leg sets with 1-1/4" pocket-hole screws and glue, joining them into the full leg base.

steps.title: Attach the seat back
steps.body: Attach the seat back to the seat. If you're painting, pocket holes work well (3/4" setting, 1-1/4" pocket-hole screws). If you're staining, attach from the underside instead, with carefully placed and predrilled 2-1/2" screws, so the show faces stay clean.

steps.title: Attach the seat to the base
steps.body: Attach the seat to the leg base through the pocket holes drilled in the previous two steps.

images.alt: Small splay-legged kids stool with a seat back
